item.title: [9] NMR evidence for an antisite-induced magnetic moment on Bi in the topological insulator heterostructure MnBi2Te4/(Bi2Te3)n
item.description:
Last authors: A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik

Authors: R. Kalvig, E. Jedryka, A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental NMR study of intrinsic magnetic topological insulator MnBi2Te4 heterostructure.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #magnetictopologicalmaterials #heterostructure #NMR

Abstract: Author(s): R. Kalvig, E. Jedryka, A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ (MBT) is the first intrinsic magnetic topological insulator, combining a topologically protected surface metallic state and intrinsic magnetic order. A structural compatibility with the nonmagnetic ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ (BT) parent compound gives… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184420] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. Kalvig, E. Jedryka, A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik

MnBi2Te4 (MBT) is the first intrinsic magnetic topological insulator, combining a topologically protected surface metallic state and intrinsic magnetic order. A structural compatibility with the nonmagnetic Bi2Te3 (BT) parent compound gives a possibility to create MBT/BT heterostructures and manipul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184420] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] NMR evidence for an antisite-induced magnetic moment on Bi in the topological insulator heterostructure MnBi2Te4/(Bi2Te3)n

item.title: [8] Electric-field modulation of magnetic skyrmions in a two-dimensional multiferroic
item.description:
Last authors: Junting Zhang, Milorad V. Milošević

Authors: Xiaofan Shen, Ke Ji, Xiaomei Lu, Junting Zhang, Milorad V. Milošević

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of electric-field control of topological magnetic skyrmions in a 2D multiferroic heterostructure.

Tags: #topological #magneticmaterials #heterostructure

Abstract: Author(s): Xiaofan Shen, Ke Ji, Xiaomei Lu, Junting Zhang, and Milorad V. Milošević Magnetoelectric coupling in multiferroics provides a low-energy means of manipulating magnetic spin textures, including topological ones such as skyrmions, using an electric field instead of a current. However, existing demonstrations were realized in multiferroic heterostructures, relying on ferroe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184422] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaofan Shen, Ke Ji, Xiaomei Lu, Junting Zhang, and Milorad V. Milošević

Magnetoelectric coupling in multiferroics provides a low-energy means of manipulating magnetic spin textures, including topological ones such as skyrmions, using an electric field instead of a current. However, existing demonstrations were realized in multiferroic heterostructures, relying on ferroe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184422] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Electric-field modulation of magnetic skyrmions in a two-dimensional multiferroic
item.dc:creator: Xiaofan Shen, Ke Ji, Xiaomei Lu, Junting Zhang, Milorad V. Milošević

item.title: [8] Quantum altermagnetic instability in disordered metals
item.description:
Last authors: Alberto Cortijo

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of quantum altermagnetic instability in disordered metals, direct interest for altermagnetism.

Tags: #altermagnetism #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Alberto Cortijo The possibility of a zero-temperature altermagnetic instability in anisotropic two-dimensional electron systems in the diffusive regime is analyzed in the presence and absence of spin-orbit coupling. Allowing for ferromagnetism, a phase diagram is built as a function of the parameter that controls a… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205115] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alberto Cortijo

The possibility of a zero-temperature altermagnetic instability in anisotropic two-dimensional electron systems in the diffusive regime is analyzed in the presence and absence of spin-orbit coupling. Allowing for ferromagnetism, a phase diagram is built as a function of the parameter that controls a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205115] Published Wed May 06, 2026

item.description:
Last authors: Chen-Xin Jiang, Zi-Xiang Hu

Why: Study of dynamics in fractional quantum Hall liquids, a broader interest.

Tags: #quantumHall #fractionalquantumHall

Abstract: Author(s): Jie Li, Chen-Xin Jiang, and Zi-Xiang Hu Motivated by recent experimental advancements in scanning optical stroboscopic confocal microscopy and spectroscopy measurements, which have facilitated exceptional energy-space-time resolution for investigating edge and bulk dynamics in fractional quantum Hall systems, we formulated a model for the… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205117] Published Wed May 06, 2026

Author(s): Jie Li, Chen-Xin Jiang, and Zi-Xiang Hu

Motivated by recent experimental advancements in scanning optical stroboscopic confocal microscopy and spectroscopy measurements, which have facilitated exceptional energy-space-time resolution for investigating edge and bulk dynamics in fractional quantum Hall systems, we formulated a model for the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205117] Published Wed May 06, 2026

item.title: [8] Disorder-induced strong-field strong localization in two-dimensional systems
item.description:
Last authors: Yi Huang, Sankar Das Sarma

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental STM study on bilayer graphene in strong magnetic fields, probing quantum phases in Landau levels.

Tags: #STM #graphene #quantumHall #Landaulevel

Abstract: Author(s): Yi Huang and Sankar Das Sarma A recent STM experiment in two-dimensional bilayer graphene [Y.-C. Tsui et al. , Nature (London) 628 , 287 (2024) ], under a strong perpendicular magnetic field, has made a direct observation of the existence of three distinct filling-factor-dependent quantum phases in the lowest Landau level: the inc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184203] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi Huang and Sankar Das Sarma

A recent STM experiment in two-dimensional bilayer graphene [Y.-C. Tsui et al., Nature (London) 628, 287 (2024)], under a strong perpendicular magnetic field, has made a direct observation of the existence of three distinct filling-factor-dependent quantum phases in the lowest Landau level: the inc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184203] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [7] Landauer-based study of transport in Chern insulator heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: J. Luna-Ramos, A. Martín-Ruiz

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of transport in Chern insulator heterostructures, relevant to topological materials.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #Cherninsulator #heterostructure #transport

Abstract: Author(s): J. Luna-Ramos and A. Martín-Ruiz We study charge transport through a trivial-topological-trivial junction described by the continuous Qi-Wu-Zhang model, which realizes a two-dimensional Chern-insulating phase. The central region is tuned into the topological regime, while the adjoining leads remain trivial, and an electrostatic bar… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184204] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. Luna-Ramos and A. Martín-Ruiz

We study charge transport through a trivial-topological-trivial junction described by the continuous Qi-Wu-Zhang model, which realizes a two-dimensional Chern-insulating phase. The central region is tuned into the topological regime, while the adjoining leads remain trivial, and an electrostatic bar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184204] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [7] Classical symmetry enriched topological orders and distinct monopole charges for dipole-octupole spin ices
item.description:
Last authors: Pengwei Zhao, Gang v. Chen

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of symmetry-enriched topological orders in pyrochlore spin ice materials.

Tags: #topological #spinliquid #stronglycorrelatedelectronsystems #magneticmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Pengwei Zhao and Gang v. Chen Distinct symmetry enriched topological orders often do not have classical distinctions. Motivated by the recent progress on the pyrochlore spin ice materials based on the dipole-octupole doublets, we argue that the dipolar spin liquid and the octupolar spin liquid can be distinguished through the ma… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184417] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pengwei Zhao and Gang v. Chen

Distinct symmetry enriched topological orders often do not have classical distinctions. Motivated by the recent progress on the pyrochlore spin ice materials based on the dipole-octupole doublets, we argue that the dipolar spin liquid and the octupolar spin liquid can be distinguished through the ma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184417] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [9] Intrinsic negative magnetoresistance in layered antiferromagnetic semimetals: The case of EuSn2As2
item.description:
Last authors: V. A. Golyashov, V. M. Pudalov

Authors: K. S. Pervakov, A. V. Sadakov, O. A. Sobolevskiy, et al.

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: Direct ARPES experimental study on a layered antiferromagnetic semimetal with transport and DFT.

Tags: #ARPES #antiferromagnet #semimetal #transport #DFT

Abstract: Author(s): K. S. Pervakov, A. V. Sadakov, O. A. Sobolevskiy, V. A. Vlasenko, V. P. Martovitsky, E. A. Sedov, E. I. Maltsev, P. D. Grigoriev, N. S. Pavlov, I. A. Nekrasov, O. E. Tereshchenko, V. A. Golyashov, and V. M. Pudalov Here, by applying a comprehensive approach including magnetic, transport measurements, ARPES band structure measurements, DFT calculations, and analytical theory consideration, we unveil the puzzling origin of the negative isotropic magnetoresistance in the highly anisotropic semimetals, particularl… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205111] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): K. S. Pervakov, A. V. Sadakov, O. A. Sobolevskiy, V. A. Vlasenko, V. P. Martovitsky, E. A. Sedov, E. I. Maltsev, P. D. Grigoriev, N. S. Pavlov, I. A. Nekrasov, O. E. Tereshchenko, V. A. Golyashov, and V. M. Pudalov

Here, by applying a comprehensive approach including magnetic, transport measurements, ARPES band structure measurements, DFT calculations, and analytical theory consideration, we unveil the puzzling origin of the negative isotropic magnetoresistance in the highly anisotropic semimetals, particularl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205111] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [9] Emergent one-dimensional Luttinger liquid states at charge density wave domain walls embedded in monolayer 1T−VSe2
item.description:
Last authors: Lijie Zhang, Zhihui Qin

Authors: Chen Zhang, Sahr Izadi Vishkayi, Qiwei Tian, et al.

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: Luttinger liquid

Tags: #chargedensitywave #Luttinger #monolayer #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Chen Zhang, Sahr Izadi Vishkayi, Qiwei Tian, Yulong Zeng, Bo Li, Linghui Tong, Yang Zhang, Li Zhang, Yuan Tian, Long-Jing Yin, Meysam Bagheri Tagani, Lijie Zhang, and Zhihui Qin Beyond the Landau Fermi liquid paradigm, non-Fermi liquid behavior stands as the cornerstone of deciphering emergent phenomena in strongly correlated quantum systems. In one-dimensional (1D) systems, the Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid (TLL) theory predicts exotic collective excitations and spin-charge se… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205113] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chen Zhang, Sahr Izadi Vishkayi, Qiwei Tian, Yulong Zeng, Bo Li, Linghui Tong, Yang Zhang, Li Zhang, Yuan Tian, Long-Jing Yin, Meysam Bagheri Tagani, Lijie Zhang, and Zhihui Qin

Beyond the Landau Fermi liquid paradigm, non-Fermi liquid behavior stands as the cornerstone of deciphering emergent phenomena in strongly correlated quantum systems. In one-dimensional (1D) systems, the Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid (TLL) theory predicts exotic collective excitations and spin-charge se…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205113] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [7] Drift and Hall mobility of graphene-like Dirac materials
item.description:
Last authors: Yingqi Wang, Zhirong Liu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of Hall effect and mobility in graphene-like Dirac materials, relevant to transport.

Tags: #graphene #Dirac #Hall_effect #transport_theory

Abstract: Author(s): Yingqi Wang and Zhirong Liu In experiments, the carrier mobility of materials is usually evaluated based on Hall effect measurements. For most semiconductors, the mobility obtained by this method (Hall mobility) is a good estimate of the drift mobility. However, in this work we find that for Dirac materials such as graphene, t… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205408] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yingqi Wang and Zhirong Liu

In experiments, the carrier mobility of materials is usually evaluated based on Hall effect measurements. For most semiconductors, the mobility obtained by this method (Hall mobility) is a good estimate of the drift mobility. However, in this work we find that for Dirac materials such as graphene, t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205408] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [8] Sliding-driven switchable layer-polarized anomalous Hall effect in inversion-symmetric van der Waals systems
item.description:
Last authors: Yehui Zhang, Liang Ma

Authors: Xinyang Li, Yehui Zhang, Liang Ma

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of anomalous Hall effect in topological van der Waals systems, fits broader interests.

Tags: #anomalous_Hall #van_der_Waals #topological #Hall_effect

Abstract: Author(s): Xinyang Li, Yehui Zhang, and Liang Ma The layer-polarized anomalous Hall effect (LP-AHE), observed in topological systems and inversion-asymmetric valleytronic systems, has attracted significant interest. However, its practical application is limited by stringent requirements of either a continuously applied external electric field or t… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205409] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xinyang Li, Yehui Zhang, and Liang Ma

The layer-polarized anomalous Hall effect (LP-AHE), observed in topological systems and inversion-asymmetric valleytronic systems, has attracted significant interest. However, its practical application is limited by stringent requirements of either a continuously applied external electric field or t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205409] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [7] Spiral spin liquid resilient to quantization in the frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet GdZnPO
item.description:
Last authors: Yi Cui, Yuesheng Li

Authors: Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental NMR study on spin liquid behavior in a frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet.

Tags: #spin_liquid #antiferromagnet #NMR #quantum_magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, Haijun Liao, Weiqiang Yu, Yi Cui, and Yuesheng Li Spin liquids usually emerge in low-spin ( S =½ or 1) systems with strong quantum fluctuations. Here, the authors show that GdZnPO—a structurally disorder-free, high-spin ( S =7⁄2) frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet—defies this expectation. Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) measurements reveal persistent spin dynamics and spin-liquid behavior down to the lowest accessible temperatures in GdZnPO. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, Haijun Liao, Weiqiang Yu, Yi Cui, and Yuesheng Li

Spin liquids usually emerge in low-spin (S=½ or 1) systems with strong quantum fluctuations. Here, the authors show that GdZnPO—a structurally disorder-free, high-spin (S=7⁄2) frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet—defies this expectation. Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) measurements reveal persistent spin dynamics and spin-liquid behavior down to the lowest accessible temperatures in GdZnPO.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Spiral spin liquid resilient to quantization in the frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet GdZnPO
item.dc:creator: Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Ginzburg-Landau theory of Rashba superconductors: Concave upper critical field
item.description:
Last authors: Qihong Chen, Kui Jin

Authors: Fedor V. Kusmartsev, Anna Kusmartseva, Jack C. M. Hughes, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Ginzburg-Landau theory for Rashba and topological superconductors, interpreting experimental Hc2.

Tags: #superconductivity_theory #Rashba_SOC #topological_superconductor

Abstract: Author(s): Fedor V. Kusmartsev, Anna Kusmartseva, Jack C. M. Hughes, Fatemah H. Alkallas, Amira Ben Gouider Trabelsi, Qihong Chen, and Kui Jin Broken inversion symmetry together with Rashba spin-orbit coupling fundamentally alters how superconductivity withstands applied magnetic fields. This work constructs a symmetry-respecting Ginzburg–Landau framework for Rashba superconductors and demonstrates that the upper critical field H c 2 ( T ) exhibits a distinctly concave temperature dependence in contrast to the usual saturationlike behavior of conventional superconductors. Within this theory, the interplay between Rashba coupling and the effective mass of Cooper pairs is shown to control the superconducting phase boundary, yielding a compact analytic tool for interpreting and fitting unusually high critical fields in noncentrosymmetric systems. The approach further reveals that parity-mixed, multicomponent order parameters can host soft relative-phase modes with axionlike properties, thereby connecting the physics of the phase boundary to topological superconductivity, spintronics, axion dark matter, and the targeted design of future quantum materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 179601] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fedor V. Kusmartsev, Anna Kusmartseva, Jack C. M. Hughes, Fatemah H. Alkallas, Amira Ben Gouider Trabelsi, Qihong Chen, and Kui Jin

Broken inversion symmetry together with Rashba spin-orbit coupling fundamentally alters how superconductivity withstands applied magnetic fields. This work constructs a symmetry-respecting Ginzburg–Landau framework for Rashba superconductors and demonstrates that the upper critical field Hc2(T) exhibits a distinctly concave temperature dependence in contrast to the usual saturationlike behavior of conventional superconductors. Within this theory, the interplay between Rashba coupling and the effective mass of Cooper pairs is shown to control the superconducting phase boundary, yielding a compact analytic tool for interpreting and fitting unusually high critical fields in noncentrosymmetric systems. The approach further reveals that parity-mixed, multicomponent order parameters can host soft relative-phase modes with axionlike properties, thereby connecting the physics of the phase boundary to topological superconductivity, spintronics, axion dark matter, and the targeted design of future quantum materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 179601] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Ginzburg-Landau theory of Rashba superconductors: Concave upper critical field
item.dc:creator: Fedor V. Kusmartsev, Anna Kusmartseva, Jack C. M. Hughes, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Technical Perspective
item.prism:section: Technical Perspective

item.title: [7] Pressure-induced spin reorientation and isostructural phase transition in Sm0.5Y0.5FeO3
item.description:
Last authors: Hongliang Dong, Bin Chen

Authors: Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of spin reorientation in a strongly correlated ferromagnet.

Tags: #strongly_correlated #ferromagnet #phase_transition

Abstract: Author(s): Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, Xuefeng Zhou, Zhikai Zhu, Yi Yang, Zihua Wu, Yiming Cao, Dayong Tan, Anwar Hushur, Qingyang Hu, Min Gao, Hongliang Dong, and Bin Chen The rare-earth orthoferrite family is prototypical strongly correlated systems, known for its highly tunable ferromagnetism properties. Among these materials, $\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{m}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Y}}_{0.5}\mathrm{Fe}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$ exhibits potential for applications becaue of its near room tempe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184104] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, Xuefeng Zhou, Zhikai Zhu, Yi Yang, Zihua Wu, Yiming Cao, Dayong Tan, Anwar Hushur, Qingyang Hu, Min Gao, Hongliang Dong, and Bin Chen

The rare-earth orthoferrite family is prototypical strongly correlated systems, known for its highly tunable ferromagnetism properties. Among these materials, Sm0.5Y0.5FeO3 exhibits potential for applications becaue of its near room temperature spin reorientation transition. However, a comprehensive…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184104] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Pressure-induced spin reorientation and isostructural phase transition in Sm0.5Y0.5FeO3
item.dc:creator: Chao Qian, Wenming Qi, Jun Deng, et al.

item.title: [8] Mechanism of anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in quasi-two-dimensional MnAlGe epitaxial thin films
item.description:
Last authors: Taisuke T. Sasaki, Yuya Sakuraba

Authors: Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental and theoretical study of anomalous Hall and Nernst effects driven by Berry curvature.

Tags: #anomalous_Hall #Berry_curvature #DFT #thin_film

Abstract: Author(s): Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, Benugopal Bairagya, Keisuke Masuda, Weinan Zhou, Taisuke T. Sasaki, and Yuya Sakuraba We report a comprehensive experimental and theoretical study of the anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in (001)-oriented epitaxial MnAlGe thin films. First-principles calculations of intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity originating from Berry curvature $({σ}_{xy}^{int})$ predict a large value, $∼856… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, Benugopal Bairagya, Keisuke Masuda, Weinan Zhou, Taisuke T. Sasaki, and Yuya Sakuraba

We report a comprehensive experimental and theoretical study of the anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in (001)-oriented epitaxial MnAlGe thin films. First-principles calculations of intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity originating from Berry curvature (σxyint) predict a large value, ∼856Scm−1, at 0…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Mechanism of anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in quasi-two-dimensional MnAlGe epitaxial thin films
item.dc:creator: Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [7] Tunable magnetic excitations in the honeycomb antiferromagnet (Co,Ni)TiO3
item.description:
Last authors: Stuart Calder, Despina Louca

Authors: Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, Despina Louca

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Neutron scattering study of excitations in a honeycomb lattice magnet.

Tags: #neutronscattering #antiferromagnet #honeycomb

Abstract: Author(s): Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, and Despina Louca The solid solution of the honeycomb antiferromagnet (AFM) ${\mathrm{Co}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{TiO}}_{3}$ (CNTO) was synthesized by mixing a 1:1 molar ratio of ${\mathrm{CoTiO}}_{3}$ (CTO) and ${\mathrm{NiTiO}}_{3}$ (NTO). Powder neutron scattering was used to determine the structure and … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184412] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, and Despina Louca

The solid solution of the honeycomb antiferromagnet (AFM) Co0.5Ni0.5TiO3 (CNTO) was synthesized by mixing a 1:1 molar ratio of CoTiO3 (CTO) and NiTiO3 (NTO). Powder neutron scattering was used to determine the structure and magnetic spectrum, where the nuclear and magnetic structures resemble those …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184412] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Tunable magnetic excitations in the honeycomb antiferromagnet (Co,Ni)TiO3
item.dc:creator: Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, Despina Louca
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [8] Theoretical design of the large topological magnetoelectric effect in the Co-intercalated NbS2 structure
item.description:
Last authors: Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Focuses on AHE and scalar spin chirality in an intercalated TMD system.

Tags: #anomalousHall #topological #NbS2

Abstract: Author(s): Hyowon Park and Ivar Martin A triangular Co-ion lattice intercalated between 1-H ${\mathrm{NbS}}_{2}$ layers can exhibit a large anomalous Hall effect (AHE) due to the finite scalar spin chirality originating from the noncoplanar $3q$ ordering of Co spins. This large AHE occurs when the scalar spin chirality is uniform in all … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184413] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hyowon Park and Ivar Martin

A triangular Co-ion lattice intercalated between 1-H NbS2 layers can exhibit a large anomalous Hall effect (AHE) due to the finite scalar spin chirality originating from the noncoplanar 3q ordering of Co spins. This large AHE occurs when the scalar spin chirality is uniform in all Co layers, as inde…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184413] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Theoretical design of the large topological magnetoelectric effect in the Co-intercalated NbS2 structure
item.dc:creator: Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Quasiclassical theory of vortex states in locally noncentrosymmetric superconductors: Application to CeRh2As2
item.description:
Last authors: Akihiro Minamide, Youichi Yanase

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory for a recent heavy-fermion superconductor with multi-phase SC diagram.

Tags: #heavy-fermion #superconductivity #CeRh2As2

Abstract: Author(s): Akihiro Minamide and Youichi Yanase ${\mathrm{CeRh}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$, a heavy-fermion superconductor discovered in 2021, exhibits two distinct superconducting phases under a $c$-axis magnetic field. This unconventional phase diagram has been attributed to the local inversion symmetry breaking at the $\mathrm{Ce}$ sites. At low m… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184503] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Akihiro Minamide and Youichi Yanase

CeRh2As2, a heavy-fermion superconductor discovered in 2021, exhibits two distinct superconducting phases under a c-axis magnetic field. This unconventional phase diagram has been attributed to the local inversion symmetry breaking at the Ce sites. At low magnetic fields, a conventional even-parity …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184503] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Quasiclassical theory of vortex states in locally noncentrosymmetric superconductors: Application to CeRh2As2
item.dc:creator: Akihiro Minamide, Youichi Yanase
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [8] Unconventional superconductivity in La3Ni2O7 from the perspective of symmetry
item.description:
Last authors: Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou

Authors: Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Symmetry analysis of high-Tc nickelate superconductors, a high-priority topic.

Tags: #nickelate #superconductivity #unconventional

Abstract: Author(s): Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, and Yusheng Hou The recently discovered superconductor ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ has attracted significant attention due to its remarkably high transition temperature (${T}_{c}$) under high pressure. Shortly after this discovery, thin-film ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184505] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, and Yusheng Hou

The recently discovered superconductor La3Ni2O7 has attracted significant attention due to its remarkably high transition temperature (Tc) under high pressure. Shortly after this discovery, thin-film La3Ni2O7 was demonstrated to exhibit ambient-pressure superconductivity; however, the corresponding …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184505] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Unconventional superconductivity in La3Ni2O7 from the perspective of symmetry
item.dc:creator: Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou

item.title: [8] Superconductivity in UTe2 from local noncentrosymmetricity
item.description:
Last authors: Ryuji Hakuno, Youichi Yanase

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Discusses pairing symmetry in the major spin-triplet candidate UTe2.

Tags: #superconductivity #UTe2 #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Ryuji Hakuno and Youichi Yanase Superconductivity in ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$ has attracted significant attention, as it is widely recognized as a promising candidate for a spin-triplet superconductor. However, the symmetry of superconductivity and the microscopic origin of spin-triplet pairing remain subjects of debate. Nevertheless,… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195107] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryuji Hakuno and Youichi Yanase

Superconductivity in UTe2 has attracted significant attention, as it is widely recognized as a promising candidate for a spin-triplet superconductor. However, the symmetry of superconductivity and the microscopic origin of spin-triplet pairing remain subjects of debate. Nevertheless, various experim…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195107] Published Mon May 04, 2026

item.title: [8] Coexistence of moiré potential and electrostatic potential in twisted bilayer graphene/WSe2 heterostructure quantum dots
item.description:
Last authors: Xiao-Feng Zhou, Lin He

Authors: Hao Sheng, Ke Lv, Mo-Han Zhang, Ya-Ning Ren, Xiao-Feng Zhou, Lin He

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Moire/twisted bilayer graphene heterostructure experiment, relevant to electronic states and quantum confinement.

Tags: #graphene #moire #heterostructure

Abstract: Author(s): Hao Sheng, Ke Lv, Mo-Han Zhang, Ya-Ning Ren, Xiao-Feng Zhou, and Lin He Twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) emerges as an ideal platform for engineering electronic states owing to its moiré superlattice and tunable electronic structure. Although the intrinsic properties of TBG have been widely studied, its electronic behavior under quantum confinement remains largely unexplo… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195403] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao Sheng, Ke Lv, Mo-Han Zhang, Ya-Ning Ren, Xiao-Feng Zhou, and Lin He

Twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) emerges as an ideal platform for engineering electronic states owing to its moiré superlattice and tunable electronic structure. Although the intrinsic properties of TBG have been widely studied, its electronic behavior under quantum confinement remains largely unexplo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195403] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Coexistence of moiré potential and electrostatic potential in twisted bilayer graphene/WSe2 heterostructure quantum dots

item.title: [7] Chiral edge plasmons in quantum anomalous Hall insulators
item.description:
Last authors: Jianhui Zhou, Yugui Yao

Authors: Furu Zhang, Chenxi Ding, Jianhui Zhou, Yugui Yao

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of Berry curvature effects on edge modes in QAH systems.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #anomalousHall #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Furu Zhang, Chenxi Ding, Jianhui Zhou, and Yugui Yao We find that the Berry curvature splits the edge plasmons propagating along the opposite directions in quantum anomalous Hall insulators even with vanishing Chern number. When the bulk is insulating, a single unidirectional edge plasmon survives and becomes acoustic in the long-wavelength limit, whi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Furu Zhang, Chenxi Ding, Jianhui Zhou, and Yugui Yao

We find that the Berry curvature splits the edge plasmons propagating along the opposite directions in quantum anomalous Hall insulators even with vanishing Chern number. When the bulk is insulating, a single unidirectional edge plasmon survives and becomes acoustic in the long-wavelength limit, whi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Chiral edge plasmons in quantum anomalous Hall insulators

item.title: [7] Twisted bilayer graphene as a terahertz plasmonic crystal
item.description:
Last authors: Brian S. Vermilyea, Michael M. Fogler

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Explores electronic states in twisted bilayer graphene moire crystals, a strong area of interest.

Tags: #graphene #moire #twistedbilayer #plasmonics

Abstract: Author(s): Brian S. Vermilyea and Michael M. Fogler We study surface plasmons in minimally twisted gapped bilayer graphene that contains a triangular network of partial dislocations (or AB-BA domain walls) hosting topologically protected one-dimensional electronic states. We show that this system behaves as a plasmonic crystal and we calculate its ba… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Brian S. Vermilyea and Michael M. Fogler

We study surface plasmons in minimally twisted gapped bilayer graphene that contains a triangular network of partial dislocations (or AB-BA domain walls) hosting topologically protected one-dimensional electronic states. We show that this system behaves as a plasmonic crystal and we calculate its ba…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Twisted bilayer graphene as a terahertz plasmonic crystal

item.title: [8] Engineering subgap states in superconductors by the symmetry of altermagnetism
item.description:
Last authors: Yukio Tanaka, Yuri Fukaya

Authors: Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of altermagnet-superconductor heterostructures and their spectroscopic signals, highly relevant to altermagnetism interests.

Tags: #altermagnetism #superconductivity #subgapstates #spectroscopy

Abstract: Author(s): Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, Keiji Yada, Jorge Cayao, Yukio Tanaka, and Yuri Fukaya The authors demonstrate here that altermagnetism is a powerful phenomenon for realizing highly tunable subgap states in unconventional superconductors at zero net magnetization. In particular, they unveil bulk and surface subgap states exhibiting distinct highly tunable dispersions, which are entirely controlled by altermagnetic symmetries. These subgap states are further shown to induce direct measurable conductance signals, thereby offering a way to detect altermagnetism and superconductivity. These results establish altermagnetism as a versatile mechanism for functionalizing superconducting devices. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180501] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, Keiji Yada, Jorge Cayao, Yukio Tanaka, and Yuri Fukaya

The authors demonstrate here that altermagnetism is a powerful phenomenon for realizing highly tunable subgap states in unconventional superconductors at zero net magnetization. In particular, they unveil bulk and surface subgap states exhibiting distinct highly tunable dispersions, which are entirely controlled by altermagnetic symmetries. These subgap states are further shown to induce direct measurable conductance signals, thereby offering a way to detect altermagnetism and superconductivity. These results establish altermagnetism as a versatile mechanism for functionalizing superconducting devices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L180501] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Engineering subgap states in superconductors by the symmetry of altermagnetism
item.dc:creator: Bo Lu, Phillip Mercebach, Pablo Burset, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [7] Quantum geometric origin of the intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect
item.description:
Last authors: Laura Classen, Andreas P. Schnyder

Authors: Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, Andreas P. Schnyder

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Connects quantum geometry and transport near topological crossings, relevant to topological semimetal interests.

Tags: #topological #Halleffect #quantumgeometry

Abstract: Author(s): Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, and Andreas P. Schnyder Quantum geometry, which encodes the momentum-space structure of Bloch electrons in crystals, both governs nonlinear transport and can be probed through it. Using a systematic, gauge-invariant projector formalism, the authors develop here a quantum-geometric classification of the nonlinear Hall effect. They identify a previously overlooked intraband quantum metric dipole, disentangle contributions by symmetry, and clarify inconsistencies in earlier formulations. The work highlights nonlinear transport as a sensitive probe of quantum geometry, especially near topological band crossings. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201107] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, and Andreas P. Schnyder

Quantum geometry, which encodes the momentum-space structure of Bloch electrons in crystals, both governs nonlinear transport and can be probed through it. Using a systematic, gauge-invariant projector formalism, the authors develop here a quantum-geometric classification of the nonlinear Hall effect. They identify a previously overlooked intraband quantum metric dipole, disentangle contributions by symmetry, and clarify inconsistencies in earlier formulations. The work highlights nonlinear transport as a sensitive probe of quantum geometry, especially near topological band crossings.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L201107] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Quantum geometric origin of the intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect
item.dc:creator: Yannis Ulrich, Johannes Mitscherling, Laura Classen, Andreas P. Schnyder
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: [7] Sliding-driven spin-polarized transport of magnon orbital moments in an altermagnet
item.description:
Last authors: Fawei Zheng, Yugui Yao

Authors: Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, Yugui Yao

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of magnon transport in altermagnets, matching a listed material project interest.

Tags: #altermagnetism #transport #Berrycurvature

Abstract: Author(s): Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao Here, we propose a method to introduce the magnon orbital Nernst effect in twisted collinear magnets through broken spatial inversion symmetry. We demonstrate that nonzero Berry curvature and orbital moments of magnons can be achieved by atomic translation, including spin-polarized transport of magn… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao

Here, we propose a method to introduce the magnon orbital Nernst effect in twisted collinear magnets through broken spatial inversion symmetry. We demonstrate that nonzero Berry curvature and orbital moments of magnons can be achieved by atomic translation, including spin-polarized transport of magn…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Sliding-driven spin-polarized transport of magnon orbital moments in an altermagnet

item.title: [7] Helical phases and Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces probed by superconducting diode effects
item.description:
Last authors: Jaglul Hasan, Alex Levchenko

Authors: Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, Alex Levchenko

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigates Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces, an electronic structure feature highly relevant to ARPES specialists.

Tags: #superconductivity #Fermisurface #helicalphase

Abstract: Author(s): Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko Noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NCSs) with Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and in-plane magnetic fields have emerged as natural platforms for realizing both the bulk superconducting diode effect (SDE) and the Josephson diode effect (JDE)—phenomena characterized by unequal critical currents in o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174501] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko

Noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NCSs) with Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and in-plane magnetic fields have emerged as natural platforms for realizing both the bulk superconducting diode effect (SDE) and the Josephson diode effect (JDE)—phenomena characterized by unequal critical currents in o…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174501] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Helical phases and Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces probed by superconducting diode effects

item.title: [10] Josephson spectroscopy study of kagome superconductors toward the deep point-contact regime
item.description:
Last authors: Yuanyuan Zhao, Jia-Xin Yin

Authors: Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, et al.

Score 10/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct JSTM study of kagome superconductors, aligning perfectly with projects on AV3Sb5 materials.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #kagome #STM #superconductivity #AV3Sb5

Abstract: Author(s): Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin Josephson scanning tunneling microscopy (JSTM) has emerged as an important technique for probing the superconducting order parameter at the atomic scale. However, the Josephson current in JSTM may behave quite differently when the coupling strength varies. Here, we push the junction to the deep poin… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174502] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin

Josephson scanning tunneling microscopy (JSTM) has emerged as an important technique for probing the superconducting order parameter at the atomic scale. However, the Josephson current in JSTM may behave quite differently when the coupling strength varies. Here, we push the junction to the deep poin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174502] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [7] Slow-phonon control of spin Edelstein effect in Rashba d-wave altermagnets
item.description:
Last authors: Jacob Linder, James K. Freericks

Authors: Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, James K. Freericks

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of altermagnetism and spin polarization, relevant to material interests.

Tags: #altermagnetism #phonons #Rashba

Abstract: Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks Altermagnets have zero net magnetization yet feature spin-split bands. Here, we investigate how slow lattice vibrations (phonons) influence both the intrinsic and externally induced spin polarizations in two-dimensional $d$-wave altermagnets. For the induced spin polarization, we employ a Rashba con… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184403] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks

Altermagnets have zero net magnetization yet feature spin-split bands. Here, we investigate how slow lattice vibrations (phonons) influence both the intrinsic and externally induced spin polarizations in two-dimensional d-wave altermagnets. For the induced spin polarization, we employ a Rashba conti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184403] Published Fri May 01, 2026

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: DFT+DMFT study of correlations in cuprates and nickelates, matching listed broad interests.

Tags: #cuprates #nickelates #DFT+DMFT #correlations

Abstract: Author(s): Jian-Hong She, Jing-Xuan Wang, Rong-Qiang He, and Zhong-Yi Lu The recent discovery of high-temperature superconductivity in the bilayer nickelate ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ has spurred intense interest in exploring analogous mechanisms in other transition metal oxides. This raises a pivotal question: can cuprates, as neighbors to nick… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195101] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jian-Hong She, Jing-Xuan Wang, Rong-Qiang He, and Zhong-Yi Lu

The recent discovery of high-temperature superconductivity in the bilayer nickelate La3Ni2O7 has spurred intense interest in exploring analogous mechanisms in other transition metal oxides. This raises a pivotal question: can cuprates, as neighbors to nickelates in the periodic table, host similar t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195101] Published Fri May 01, 2026

Last authors: Xiangang Wan, Di Wang

Authors: Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigates magnetoelectric coupling in altermagnetic systems, a core interest area.

Tags: #altermagnetism #multiferroics #magnetoelectric

Abstract: Author(s): Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang Type-II multiferroics offer a promising route to strong magnetoelectric coupling due to the magnetic-order-driven ferroelectricity. Most experimentally realized type-II multiferroics exhibit noncollinear magnetic order, whose complex interaction with external fields can impede the practical control … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195105] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang

Type-II multiferroics offer a promising route to strong magnetoelectric coupling due to the magnetic-order-driven ferroelectricity. Most experimentally realized type-II multiferroics exhibit noncollinear magnetic order, whose complex interaction with external fields can impede the practical control …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195105] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [7] Hund's metal physics in uranium mononitride
item.description:
Last authors: Mark R. Pederson, Eunja Kim

Authors: Byungkyun Kang, Stephen S. Micklo, Roy N. Herrera-Navarro, Mark R. Pederson, Eunja Kim

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: DFT+DMFT analysis of heavy fermion behavior, matching broad electronic structure interests.

Tags: #heavyfermion #Kondo #DFT+DMFT

Abstract: Author(s): Byungkyun Kang, Stephen S. Micklo, Roy N. Herrera-Navarro, Mark R. Pederson, and Eunja Kim Uranium $5f$ electrons often yield heavy-fermion behavior via Kondo screening. However, the pronounced bad-metallic transport of uranium mononitride (UN) defies an incoherent Kondo explanation. Using density-functional theory combined with dynamical mean-field theory, we show that UN is a strongly c… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195106] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Byungkyun Kang, Stephen S. Micklo, Roy N. Herrera-Navarro, Mark R. Pederson, and Eunja Kim

Uranium 5f electrons often yield heavy-fermion behavior via Kondo screening. However, the pronounced bad-metallic transport of uranium mononitride (UN) defies an incoherent Kondo explanation. Using density-functional theory combined with dynamical mean-field theory, we show that UN is a strongly cor…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195106] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Hund's metal physics in uranium mononitride
item.dc:creator: Byungkyun Kang, Stephen S. Micklo, Roy N. Herrera-Navarro, Mark R. Pederson, Eunja Kim
